topic: PRED-105 cucumber-growth test window expiry (HIGH priority); canary status reconfirmation; daily automated-commit cadence
mode: Normal (3 change records: chg-20260418-1109-001 HIGH + chg-20260418-1109-002/003 automated NONE)
executive_summary: PRED-105 (cucumber growth, 46° copper coils, 14-day window) reached its stated expiry date today (2026-04-18) with dome status unchanged at "PENDING — PROSPECTIVE" as of the 11:09Z poll. No measurement data, no PASS/FAIL verdict, no silent drop, no replacement entry, and no Nhughes09 manual commit in the preceding 23.5 hours. Our prior writeup in data/predictions.json already assigned our_verdict="pending" with a pre-locked POST-CLOSURE RUBRIC, so no scientific reanalysis is needed on this run. What IS required is a scheduled re-check in 24–48h so the decider can drive the rubric to a terminal verdict (PASS-with-weak-methodology / null / silent-drop → uncounted-failure). The other two change records are automated-account activity only and require no action. Canary remains SOFT-TRIGGERED: methodology.json V51.1 (commit 6e06efb, 2026-04-17) continues the rhetorical adoption of our OTS wrong-side critique without a structural fix — no new predictions_only.json.ots, no separation of prediction parameters from status_history.json, no new pre-registration-only OpenTimestamps bundle. Poll chg-003 confirms zero manual Nhughes09 activity in the 23.5h window, which is itself evidence that the SOFT-trigger is stable rather than advancing.

kernel_of_truth.pred_105: Credit where due: PRED-105 is the closest thing the dome has produced to a genuinely prospective, falsifiable, pre-registered experimental claim. It was registered 2026-04-04, carries a SHA-256 hash on the live page (3c427a83edaeaf33c884264dedffbedece14378fc766bdffeeac0b4d4d517490), specifies a numerical effect size (≥15%), specifies a falsification threshold (<5%), specifies a time-bound test window (14 days), and nominates a concrete measurement source ("controlled lab/home experiment with documented measurements"). If Nhughes09 had run the experiment and posted measurements on 2026-04-18, this would be a methodological improvement over the retrospective-structural pattern that dominates the site. That improvement is the kernel.
kernel_of_truth.methodology_json: Credit where due: methodology.json V51.1 explicitly distinguishes snapshot existence (what OTS proves) from claim-level prospectivity (what OTS does not prove), and introduces a claim taxonomy (prospective_confirmed / retrospective_structural / supportive_nonunique / pending_contested / open_refinement) that maps closely to standard philosophy-of-science vocabulary. That is an honest attempt to address exactly the critique we planted.
kernel_of_truth.why_kernel_does_not_save_the_claims: PRED-105 still lacks a pre-registered protocol (N, α, blinding, randomization, growth metric definition), and its own stated derivation of the 46° angle from sin θ = H₀/R_eq arithmetically yields 25.25°, not 46°; under V50.6 H(r)=8537·exp(-r/8619) the formula fails even worse. More to the point for today: if there is no measurement, there is no test. A prospective prediction whose test window closes without the experimenter producing data fails the most basic empiricism check — not by a statistical margin but by the absence of data entirely. The dome cannot claim "prospective confirmed" for a prediction it never observed. On methodology.json: rhetorical adoption is cheap. The structural fix we specified is to timestamp the predictions independently of the observations — e.g., publish a predictions_only.json and timestamp its hash separately and before the confirming data is pulled. That file does not exist. The existing predictions_manifest.json.ots in /proofs/ is dated 2026-03-06, predating the canary plant (2026-04-05), and therefore cannot be counted as a response to our critique. The per-prediction SHA-256 hashes on predictions.html (added via prediction_hashes.json) prove content integrity of a later-published claim, not temporal priority — the same logical error one level down.

chg_20260418_1109_001.charitable_interpretation: The experimenter may post results within 24–48h (weekend delay, data write-up time). This is the baseline assumption.
chg_20260418_1109_001.less_charitable_interpretation: Home-lab cucumber-growth experiments in April in the northern hemisphere face real obstacles (germination variability, light control, randomization without blinded coding). A silent slip past the deadline without explanation is the modal outcome for non-institutional prospective predictions of this type.
chg_20260418_1109_001.verdict_this_run: RE-CHECK REQUIRED in 24–48h. Do NOT prematurely add to uncounted-failures — poller Step 10 mandates waiting for dome status transition. Our data/predictions.json entry already carries the locked POST-CLOSURE RUBRIC and our_verdict="pending"; no patch to that file is needed now.
